deep_dive.title: Dustin: Draft-Augmented Sparse Verification for Efficient Long-Context Generation with Speculative Decoding
deep_dive.abstract: While speculative decoding improves inference throughput for multi-batch long-context Large Language Models (LLMs), its efficiency is often limited by a verification bottleneck where Key-Value (KV) cache loading dominates latency. Existing compression methods fail in this regime: static eviction incurs accuracy loss due to saliency shift, while dynamic selection introduces prohibitive computational overhead during the verification path. We propose Dustin, a sparse verification framework designed for long-context speculative decoding. Dustin integrates lookahead signals from the draft model with historical attention from the target model to identify critical tokens with high fidelity across multi-step verification windows. To reduce recomputation latency, this approach further employs a sparse estimation scheme that restricts importance scoring to a minimal subset of attention heads. Evaluations on PG-19 and LongBench with Qwen2.5-72B demonstrate that Dustin achieves a 27.85x speedup in self-attention and a 9.17x end-to-end decoding speedup at a 32k sequence length, all with negligible accuracy degradation.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: sparse verification framework for speculative decoding in long-context LLMs
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 长上下文LLM投机解码中的稀疏验证框架
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 长上下文LLM投机解码中的稀疏验证框架
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 长上下文LLM投机解码中的稀疏验证框架
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Dustin proposes a sparse verification framework for speculative decoding, integrating draft model lookahead signals to identify critical tokens, overcoming KV cache loading bottleneck in long-context generation.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 投机解码在多批次长上下文LLM中受限于KV缓存加载成为瓶颈。Dustin提出草稿增强稀疏验证框架，利用草稿模型的预读信号与历史注意力结合，高保真识别关键令牌。在长上下文生成中显著降低延迟，验证稀疏验证在投机解码中的有效性。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 投机解码在多批次长上下文LLM中受限于KV缓存加载成为瓶颈。Dustin提出草稿增强稀疏验证框架，利用草稿模型的预读信号与历史注意力结合，高保真识别关键令牌。在长上下文生成中显著降低延迟，验证稀疏验证在投机解码中的有效性。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: Papers central to 基于宽松验证的大预言模型投机解码方法, especially work that connects or combines: speculative decoding for large language models; relaxed verification mechanism in speculative decoding; approximate matching for speculative execution; relaxed acceptance criteria for token generation; lossy verification in LLM inference acceleration; accelerating LLM inference with speculative decoding; speculative decoding methods with relaxed verification for LLMs; approximate token matching in speculative decoding frameworks; improving speculative decoding efficiency via relaxed acceptance.

deep_dive.title: Forget Without Compromise: Nexus Sampling for Streaming KV-Cache Eviction Under Fixed Budgets
deep_dive.abstract: Long-context and agentic LLM workloads push the KV cache past any fixed memory budget, forcing the inference stack to permanently evict tokens at every step of a continuous-inference stream. Existing methods all share the same template, a per-step direct-attention score followed by deterministic top-$K$ selection, which converts a single below-cutoff step into an irreversible verdict and permanently erases any subtly important token that direct attention cannot single out from noise. To address this challenge, we propose Nexus Sampling, a training-free eviction method that pairs Nexus scoring, an iterative walk over direct attention that surfaces bridge tokens, with weighted reservoir sampling, which retains tokens with inclusion probability in place of deterministic top-$K$. Theoretically, we show that Nexus Sampling dominates deterministic top-$K$ in long-run survival of subtly important tokens. Empirically, at 80% KV cache eviction, Nexus Sampling matches dense attention within 1% on LongBench while outperforming top-$K$ baselines on retrieval-heavy tasks, with up to 10x smaller per-sequence cache memory.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: KV cache eviction for efficient LLM inference
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes Nexus Sampling, a training-free eviction method combining iterative attention scoring with weighted reservoir sampling to retain bridge tokens under fixed budgets, improving LLM inference efficiency.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有KV缓存驱逐方法依赖确定性注意分数组件，容易误删重要令牌。本文提出Nexus Sampling，通过迭代注意评分（Nexus评分）找出桥接令牌，并结合加权蓄水池采样保留这些令牌，从而在固定预算下提升长上下文LLM推理的效率。实验证明该方法优于现有驱逐策略。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有KV缓存驱逐方法依赖确定性注意分数组件，容易误删重要令牌。本文提出Nexus Sampling，通过迭代注意评分（Nexus评分）找出桥接令牌，并结合加权蓄水池采样保留这些令牌，从而在固定预算下提升长上下文LLM推理的效率。实验证明该方法优于现有驱逐策略。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: Papers central to 大语言模型高效推理相关的论文检索, especially work that connects or combines: efficient large language model inference techniques; KV cache optimization and management for LLM; speculative decoding for faster LLM inference; structured and unstructured pruning for LLMs; efficient attention mechanisms like FlashAttention or PagedAttention; survey on efficient inference of large language models; state of the art techniques for LLM inference acceleration; optimizing throughput and latency for LLM serving.

deep_dive.title: MVPruner: Dynamic Token Pruning for Accelerating Multi-view Vision-Language Models in Autonomous Driving
deep_dive.abstract: Vision-Language Models (VLMs) improve generalization and interpretability in autonomous driving but suffer from efficiency issues due to long visual token sequences, particularly in standard multi-view settings. Existing token pruning methods employ fixed pruning rate allocation and static importance metrics, ignoring dynamic inter-view importance differences and the evolving information importance during inference. Our analysis reveals that multi-view VLMs inherently encode task-related view priors in deeper layers and exhibit dynamic information requirements. Motivated by these findings, we propose MVPruner, a two-stage adaptive token pruning method that aligns pruning behavior with the model's dynamic information requirements. The first stage allocates pruning budgets based on the information diversity of each view, and retains tokens with consistent contribution across stages, ensuring semantic representational capacity. The second stage allocates budgets and selects tokens guided by instruction text to guarantee task alignment. Experimental results on four benchmarks demonstrate the superior performance of our method. For example, DriveMM equipped with MVPruner achieves 87.3% reduction in FLOPs, 4.97* speedup in prefilling phase while retaining 98.5% accuracy on DriveLM benchmark.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: dynamic token pruning for multi-view VLM inference acceleration
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 多视角VLM推理加速的动态令牌剪枝
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 多视角VLM推理加速的动态令牌剪枝
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 多视角VLM推理加速的动态令牌剪枝
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: MVPruner introduces a two-stage adaptive token pruning method for multi-view VLMs, using dynamic importance metrics and view priors to accelerate inference in autonomous driving.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 多视角VLM因长视觉令牌序列导致推理效率低下，现有剪枝方法使用固定分配率与静态指标。MVPruner提出两阶段自适应令牌剪枝，利用深层视图先验和动态信息需求对齐剪枝行为。在自动驾驶数据集上实现了高加速比且保持精度。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 多视角VLM因长视觉令牌序列导致推理效率低下，现有剪枝方法使用固定分配率与静态指标。MVPruner提出两阶段自适应令牌剪枝，利用深层视图先验和动态信息需求对齐剪枝行为。在自动驾驶数据集上实现了高加速比且保持精度。

quick_skim.title: CSD: Content-aware Speculative Decoding for Efficient Image Generation
quick_skim.abstract: Speculative decoding (SD) has emerged as a key solution to accelerate the inference of autoregressive models. However, in the field of image generation, it faces the challenge of low acceptance rates, and directly relaxing its criteria leads to degradation in image quality. In this paper, we propose a novel content-aware speculative decoding algorithm, termed CSD, which integrates an entropy-based probability relaxation mechanism with an optimal resampling strategy to enhance the inference efficiency for autoregressive image generation. By leveraging the informational uncertainty inherent in different regions of an image, CSD dynamically adjusts the acceptance probability of candidate tokens, increasing the acceptance rate in low-detail areas to accelerate generation. Moreover, a distribution alignment filter is introduced to ensure the output distribution to be aligned with the target model, which significantly improves the generative quality. Experiments conducted on Lumina-mGPT and Janus-Pro demonstrate that the superiority of the proposed CSD. Our source code is available at https://github.com/aderfebr/CSD.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: content-aware speculative decoding for image generation, method transferable to VLM inference acceleration
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 面向图像生成的内容感知投机解码，方法可迁移至VLM推理加速
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 面向图像生成的内容感知投机解码，方法可迁移至VLM推理加速
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 面向图像生成的内容感知投机解码，方法可迁移至VLM推理加速
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes CSD, a content-aware speculative decoding algorithm with entropy-based relaxation and optimal resampling to accelerate autoregressive image generation, achieving higher acceptance rates without quality degradation.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对自回归图像生成中投机解码接受率低的问题，提出内容感知投机解码算法CSD，通过熵基概率松弛机制和最优重采样策略动态调整候选token接受概率，在低细节区域提高接受率以加速生成，同时不牺牲图像质量。实验表明该方法能有效提升推理效率，其思想可推广至视觉语言模型的投机解码加速。

quick_skim.title: SpikeVLA: Vision-Language-Action Models with Spiking Neural Networks
quick_skim.abstract: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models have become a dominant paradigm for embodied intelligence. However, most existing approaches are built on large-scale transformers, resulting in substantial inference latency and energy consumption that limit their practical deployment in low-power, real-time scenarios. We propose SpikeVLA, a spiking VLA architecture for embodied navigation with energy-efficient inference, consisting of three key components. (i) A spiking vision encoder, Spike-V, that replaces dense continuous layers with event-driven spiking layers to reduce the energy consumption of visual representation learning. (ii) A multi-modal spiking large language model, Spike-L, that reformulates cross-modal reasoning with spiking dynamics and token-level event-driven sparsity to further lower computational cost. (iii) A spiking action policy network, Spike-A employs Laplacian-kernel population coding with a multi-layer fully connected SNN, and decodes spiking activities into stable and robust continuous control with energy-efficient inference under low-power constraints. Experiments on navigation and robotic control tasks show that SpikeVLA significantly reduces energy consumption and computational cost while maintaining competitive performance, highlighting its potential for low-power, real-time embodied intelligence.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: energy-efficient VLA architecture with spiking neural networks for inference acceleration
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 使用脉冲神经网络的节能型VLA架构用于推理加速
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 使用脉冲神经网络的节能型VLA架构用于推理加速
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 使用脉冲神经网络的节能型VLA架构用于推理加速
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: SpikeVLA introduces spiking vision encoder and spiking multimodal LLM to replace continuous layers, reducing inference latency and energy consumption for embodied navigation VLA models.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对现有视觉-语言-动作（VLA）模型基于大规模Transformer导致推理延迟和能耗高的问题，本文提出SpikeVLA架构。该架构包括脉冲视觉编码器Spike-V和多模态脉冲大语言模型Spike-L，利用事件驱动计算替代稠密连续层，显著降低能耗。实验表明，SpikeVLA在具身导航任务中能实现能效更高的推理，为低功耗实时场景下的VLA模型部署提供了可行方案。

quick_skim.title: Phase Matters: Characterizing Heterogeneous Vision-Language Inference on a Mobile SoC
quick_skim.abstract: Recent phone-class mobile SoCs expose practical NPU execution paths for on-device vision-language model (VLM) inference, but developers still lack phase-level guidance for mapping VLM pipelines across heterogeneous backends. We present a hardware-in-the-loop characterization of VLM inference on the Qualcomm SM8750 (Snapdragon 8 Elite), covering phase throughput, cache-state effects, 100-run thermal stability, energy, heterogeneous CPU/NPU pipeline configurations, and visual-token-budget sensitivity. Using FastVLM-0.5B as an end-to-end case study, together with encoder-only measurements across four architecture families, we show that phase matters: NPU execution is highly phase-dependent, delivering 1.64x speedup for prefill but only 1.18x for decode, while vision encoders achieve 20-45x speedups over CPU. These gains translate into 10.47 degrees C lower steady-state temperature and 2.52x lower energy, avoiding thermal throttling in always-on settings. Finally, we show that a four-step graph rewrite enables previously unsupported encoders, such as Phi-3.5-V, to reach the QNN path with up to 22x speedup, providing a practical porting recipe for mobile VLM deployment.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: characterization of VLM inference on mobile SoC for acceleration
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 移动SoC上VLM推理的特征分析与加速指导
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 移动SoC上VLM推理的特征分析与加速指导
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 移动SoC上VLM推理的特征分析与加速指导
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Presents hardware-in-the-loop characterization of VLM inference on Snapdragon 8 Elite, revealing phase-dependent NPU speedups (1.64x prefill vs 1.18x decode) and guiding heterogeneous pipeline mapping.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 手机级移动SoC为端侧VLM推理提供了NPU路径，但缺乏阶段级映射指导。本文对高通SM8750进行硬件在环特征分析，覆盖阶段吞吐量、缓存状态、热稳定性等。以FastVLM-0.5B为案例，表明NPU执行高度阶段依赖：预填充加速1.64倍，解码仅1.18倍。为异构VLM推理优化提供了定量参考。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 手机级移动SoC为端侧VLM推理提供了NPU路径，但缺乏阶段级映射指导。本文对高通SM8750进行硬件在环特征分析，覆盖阶段吞吐量、缓存状态、热稳定性等。以FastVLM-0.5B为案例，表明NPU执行高度阶段依赖：预填充加速1.64倍，解码仅1.18倍。为异构VLM推理优化提供了定量参考。

quick_skim.title: Push Puppet Networks: Structured Bayesian Pruning Algorithm for Language Model Compression
quick_skim.abstract: This paper presents push puppet networks, a novel Bayesian algorithm for structured pruning of large language models. The push puppet network learns a hierarchical function during training that can optimally determine specific network layers to keep for a given target size. By adding a small number of gating parameters via a hierarchical penalty function, the learned smooth function can allow for a network to be resized to very specific sizes without loading the full model into memory or requiring further post-training computation. The method compares favorably with existing approaches (SparseGPT, Wanda) at high pruning sizes (less than 50% of network structure) while realizing measurable speed-ups on conventional GPUs with PyTorch. Furthermore, push puppet networks can achieve significant speedups as candidates for speculative decoding.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: structured pruning for LLM inference acceleration
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 结构化剪枝用于大语言模型推理加速
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 结构化剪枝用于大语言模型推理加速
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 结构化剪枝用于大语言模型推理加速
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Push Puppet Networks proposes a structured Bayesian pruning algorithm that learns hierarchical gating to optimally prune LLM layers, achieving speed-ups at high pruning ratios without loading the full model.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文提出Push Puppet Networks，一种结构化贝叶斯剪枝算法。该方法通过层次化惩罚函数学习门控参数，从而在训练中确定应保留的网络层，实现针对目标大小的精确剪枝。实验表明，在高剪枝率（低于50%结构）下，该方法优于SparseGPT和Wanda，并在常规GPU上获得可测量的加速。这项工作为大语言模型的高效推理提供了新的压缩思路。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文提出Push Puppet Networks，一种结构化贝叶斯剪枝算法。该方法通过层次化惩罚函数学习门控参数，从而在训练中确定应保留的网络层，实现针对目标大小的精确剪枝。实验表明，在高剪枝率（低于50%结构）下，该方法优于SparseGPT和Wanda，并在常规GPU上获得可测量的加速。这项工作为大语言模型的高效推理提供了新的压缩思路。
